ops.title: Consider ship auto-tracking an untracked trunk bookmark
ops.description:
E2E verification of ship fetch-first + auto-rebase (2026-07-16) confirmed: in a fresh `jj git init --colocate` repo, `main@origin` is untracked, so (a) `jj git fetch` never advances local `main`, meaning ship's divergence detection can't see a remote advance, and (b) `jj git push --bookmark exact:main` fails with "Non-tracking remote bookmark main@origin exists" and jj's hint to run `jj bookmark track main --remote=origin`.

Current behavior is a loud, actionable error (pre-existing, acceptable). Candidate improvement: when the resolved target bookmark has an untracked same-name remote counterpart, ship could run `jj bookmark track <target>@<remote>` before the divergence check — fixing both the stale-divergence blind spot and the push failure in one step. This permanently changes the bookmark's fetch semantics, so it needs a deliberate decision (auto vs a hint in ship's error), not a drive-by. See note "ship exact: bookmark-name quoting — why %q everywhere is not the fix" for the sibling review findings.

ops.text: decision recorded: auto-track vs improved error text
if auto-track: covered by a TestShipJJRebase row + e2e in a fresh colocated clone
